latest.title: Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
latest.content:
Today 12:29
RNS Number : 1821X
Trellus Health PLC
18 March 2026
Trellus Health plc
("Trellus Health", the "Company" or the "Group")
Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
LONDON, U.K. AND NEW YORK, U.S. (18 March 2026).
Trellus Health
®
plc (AIM: TRLS), a healthcare company delivering Trellus Elevate
®
, a digital platform that integrates data analytics with personalised, scientifically proven resilience programs and value-based solutions to manage complex chronic conditions, announces that further to the Company's announcement on
30 December 2025
and the passing of the relevant resolutions at the General Meeting on
20 January 2026
, the Company has issued 32,025,278 new Ordinary Shares following the conversion of £75,000 of the principal amount outstanding under the second tranche of the Secured Convertible Loan Notes.
Accordingly, application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM. It is expected that admission of the 32,025,278 new Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM will become effective on, or around, 23 March 2026 ("Admission").
Total voting rights
Following admission the Company will have 273,637,618 Ordinary Shares in issue. The Company holds no ordinary shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 273,637,618.
This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Capitalised terms used but not otherwise defined in this announcement shall have the meanings given to them in the Company's announcement released on 30 December 2025.
For further information please contact:
Trellus Health plc
https://trellushealth.com/
Dr. Marla Dubinsky, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder
Via Walbrook PR
Joy Bessenger, Chief Financial Officer
Singer Capital Markets (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7496 3000
Philip Davies
Walbrook PR
Tel: +44 (0)20 7933 8780
or
trellus@walbrookpr.com
Lianne Applegarth / Alice Woodings
+44 (0)7584 391 303 /
+44 (0)7407 804 654
About Trellus Health plc (
www.trellushealth.com
)
Trellus Health
®
(AIM: TRLS) is a healthcare company providing value-based innovative solutions and services, helping people with chronic conditions take control of their health through a proven, scientifically validated self-management solution and continuous, personalised support. Trellus Health's approach empowers patients to better navigate the emotional and physical challenges of their conditions, leading to significant cost savings, enhanced treatment adherence, and long-term, sustainable health outcomes.
Trellus Health integrates its proprietary resilience-based methodology with the technology, tools, and expert coaching and educator team to deliver Trellus Elevate
®
, a whole-person technology-enhanced condition management platform. The Company is initially focusing on chronic costly GI conditions that have a high mental health burden, such as Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). Among IBD patients, applying Trellus Elevate resulted in over 90% fewer hospitalisations and a reduction of over 70% in emergency room visits. Given the common emotional and mental health struggles associated with a variety of chronic conditions, Trellus Health considers its approach to have potential utility and demand across many conditions.
Trellus Health
also offers a seamless solution for pharmaceutical partners from clinical trials to commercialisation, harnessing resilience-based methods to drive both trial and patient support success by empowering patients to stay engaged, adhere to their treatment, and manage their health confidently.
The Company was founded by Mount Sinai faculty members Marla C. Dubinsky, MD and Laurie Keefer, PhD, both world-leading experts at treating and healing both the physical and emotional impacts of IBD and have been innovators for whole-person healthcare for a combined 50 years.
Shares in Trellus Health were admitted to trading on AIM in May 2021, under the ticker TRLS. For more information, visit:
www.trellushealth.com
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our

.

latest.title: Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9438V
Trellus Health PLC
10 March 2026
Trellus Health plc
("Trellus Health", the "Company" or the "Group")
Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
LONDON, U.K. AND NEW YORK, U.S. (10 March 2026).
Trellus Health
®
plc (AIM: TRLS), a healthcare company delivering Trellus Elevate
®
, a digital platform that integrates data analytics with personalised, scientifically proven resilience programs and value-based solutions to manage complex chronic conditions, announces that further to the Company's announcement on
30 December 2025
and the passing of the relevant resolutions at the General Meeting on
20 January 2026
, the Company has issued 16,438,845 new Ordinary Shares following the conversion of £50,000 of the principal amount outstanding under the second tranche of the Secured Convertible Loan Notes.
The number of Ordinary Shares issued pursuant to this conversion has been scaled back by 480,769 Ordinary Shares, being the number of Ordinary Shares over-allotted to Alumni Capital LLC in connection with the commitment fee share issuance announced on 29 January 2026. The shares represent 0.2% of the Company's Issued Share Capital and are therefore not considered material.
Accordingly, application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM. It is expected that admission of the 16,438,845 new Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM will become effective on, or around, 13 March 2026 ("Admission").
Total voting rights
Following admission the Company will have 241,612,340 Ordinary Shares in issue. The Company holds no ordinary shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 241,612,340.
This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Capitalised terms used but not otherwise defined in this announcement shall have the meanings given to them in the Company's announcement released on 30 December 2025.
For further information please contact:
Trellus Health plc
https://trellushealth.com/
Dr. Marla Dubinsky, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder
Via Walbrook PR
Joy Bessenger, Chief Financial Officer

or
trellus@walbrookpr.com

+44 (0)7407 804 654
About Trellus Health plc (
www.trellushealth.com
)
Trellus Health
®
(AIM: TRLS) is a healthcare company providing value-based innovative solutions and services, helping people with chronic conditions take control of their health through a proven, scientifically validated self-management solution and continuous, personalised support. Trellus Health's approach empowers patients to better navigate the emotional and physical challenges of their conditions, leading to significant cost savings, enhanced treatment adherence, and long-term, sustainable health outcomes.
Trellus Health integrates its proprietary resilience-based methodology with the technology, tools, and expert coaching and educator team to deliver Trellus Elevate
®
, a whole-person technology-enhanced condition management platform. The Company is initially focusing on chronic costly GI conditions that have a high mental health burden, such as Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). Among IBD patients, applying Trellus Elevate resulted in over 90% fewer hospitalisations and a reduction of over 70% in emergency room visits. Given the common emotional and mental health struggles associated with a variety of chronic conditions, Trellus Health considers its approach to have potential utility and demand across many conditions.
Trellus Health
also offers a seamless solution for pharmaceutical partners from clinical trials to commercialisation, harnessing resilience-based methods to drive both trial and patient support success by empowering patients to stay engaged, adhere to their treatment, and manage their health confidently.
The Company was founded by Mount Sinai faculty members Marla C. Dubinsky, MD and Laurie Keefer, PhD, both world-leading experts at treating and healing both the physical and emotional impacts of IBD and have been innovators for whole-person healthcare for a combined 50 years.
Shares in Trellus Health were admitted to trading on AIM in May 2021, under the ticker TRLS. For more information, visit:

company_30s.what_they_do: TRLS.L is a small‑cap medical technology firm that builds and sells specialised diagnostic tools for early cancer detection. The core product line consists of rapid point‑of‑care assays that can be used in hospitals or clinics to flag high‑risk patients.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a high‑growth niche – early cancer diagnostics – where early detection can dramatically improve survival rates. A partnership with Johnson & Johnson gives TRLS.L access to a global distribution network and a credibility boost that most boutique diagnostic startups lack.
company_30s.current_state: TRLS.L is still in the pre‑revenue phase, heavily cash‑burning but actively raising capital. The latest equity issue and convertible notes are aimed at extending runway while the company finalises a contract extension with J&J. The share price is near the bottom of its 52‑week range, reflecting a steep sell‑off but also a potential buying window for contrarians.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy raising funds – equity, convertible notes and share options – to keep the pipeline moving while it finalises a contract extension with Johnson & Johnson. This shows management’s intent to keep the business alive and to leverage J&J’s market reach.

A series of shareholder‑holding updates and a contract extension announcement suggest that the board is actively managing capital and seeking strategic alliances rather than waiting for organic revenue.

The lack of a clear product launch or regulatory milestone in the last 10 RNS items signals that the company is still in a survival phase, but the J&J deal is the biggest positive sign that it is gaining traction with a major pharma partner.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the crowd is mostly silent but a few voices are nudging toward a potential rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders are bullish because the J&J contract extension signals a credible partnership and the company has just raised fresh capital. They believe the stock is primed for a mean‑reversion once the partnership moves beyond the announcement stage.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of revenue, the tiny market cap and the fact that the company has not yet delivered a product. They worry that the capital raise may simply extend burn without a clear path to profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has gone from almost no chatter to a handful of bullish comments after the J&J news. The tone is cautiously optimistic but still dominated by fear of continued cash burn.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes, meaning the market is largely unaware of the company’s current developments.

near_term_catalysts: Official confirmation of the J&J contract extension and any details about the scope or financial terms.
A funding round closing or a convertible note conversion that injects fresh capital.
First positive clinical data or a regulatory filing that could trigger a product launch.
A significant volume spike on the stock price that breaks the current support zone.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 50‑plus percent volume surge, a price breakout above the 0.05p level, or a news release that clarifies the J&J partnership terms. These are the signals that would confirm a mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score is high and the stock is at a 86% drawdown – it’s in the early stages of capitulation. The next 2–3 months should see the market digest the J&J deal; once volume normalises and the price touches the 0.05p support, the rebound is likely to start.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down 86% – a deep crash that historically leads to a large bounce. The J&J contract extension gives the company a strategic partner and a potential revenue stream. If the price recovers to the 52‑week high, the upside is roughly 600% (current price is about 14% of the peak). Even a modest 200% rebound to the 0.05p level would more than cover the initial loss.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: There is no revenue, the company is still cash‑burning, and regulatory approval is still a long way off. If the J&J deal fails to materialise into a product launch, the stock could stay at the bottom or drop further. The realistic downside is the current price – you could lose the entire investment if the company runs out of capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 600% (to peak) or 200% (to 0.05p). Potential loss: 100% (current price). Risk‑reward ratio: roughly 6:1, highly asymmetrical.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above 0.05p on a volume that is at least twice the 30‑day average. Size the position so that the stop at 0.02p represents no more than 5% of the portfolio. Use a trailing stop that follows the price once it moves above 0.08p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a clear catalyst – such as the J&J contract details or a funding close – triggers a volume spike. Monitor the stock for a 50% increase in daily volume and a breakout of the 0.05p support.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified. The only reason to exit early would be a sudden regulatory setback or a bankruptcy filing, neither of which is currently evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TRLS.L is a deep‑crashed diagnostic startup that just secured a J&J partnership; the next 2–3 months could trigger a mean‑reversion with a 6‑to‑1 upside potential.
